title: generate kubernetes YAML from a libpod container
body:
you may now inspect an existing libpod container and output its description
as kubernetes YAML.  This is useful for users who want to take a container
description and run it on a runtime that understands kubernetes. When you
inspect a container, the YAML description will always "wrap" it in a simple
v1.Pod description.

Tests and further documentation will be added in additional PRs.

This function should be considered very much "under heavy development" at
this point.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: Bunch of stuff but I don't think anything major
just need a couple of lgtms

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM, I think we've hit everything

I think the current agreement is to move the kube handling to a new command 
podman kube correct?  Changing this back to a [WIP] to make sure it does not get merged.

I am not sure how you intend to run this command? I think this should be a three level command like `podman container`, `podman pod`, `podman image`

podman kube generate
podman kube replay

Could you also call the primary go program kube.go, then generate kube_generate.go and kube_replay.go.

We need man pages, completions. README.md updates ...
@rhatdan i like the recommendation ... i wasn't sure you would be willing to commit to a kube sub-command but I prefer it; will do!

as for man pages and tests and etc, i would like to complete the actual pod YAML conversion (in a sep PR) before doing that work because I believe there may be some wiggling that would require rework.
@baude Just occurred to me - we should restrict container yaml generation if it has dependencies - we can't effectively package that without a pod.
Basically, if `c.Dependencies()` is not returning a 0 length array, we need to error out and tell people we can't generate YAML, because we can't capture the container it depends on

@baude, ok I can assign the tasks to others to put together the Documentation content, and it is probably a good idea to get others to play with the stuff you are working on.  But I don't want this thing released until we have proper man pages and completions.
@rhatdan I will do docs, etc with the pod output
